[observation] [info] High cohesion (1) — files are tightly interconnected
[observation] [info] No entry points means the landing page is never imported by other zones — verify that it is loaded via a separate HTML entry point in build.js rather than through the main viewer bundle.
[observation] [info] The zone contains exactly one production file and one test file; if the landing page gains additional pages or components they should remain within this zone boundary.
[observation] [info] Zero coupling and no consumers make this a fully isolated leaf zone — an intentional design boundary that prevents landing page changes from affecting the main dashboard.
[pattern] [info] landing-page has zero inbound and zero outbound cross-zone imports — it is the only zone in the web package with no coupling surface, making it safe to refactor, delete, or rebuild in isolation
[suggestion] [info] Document the build-entry isolation mechanism explicitly in the zone's governance notes: the landing page is isolated by having a separate HTML entry point in build.js, not by import graph discipline. This prevents future maintainers from accidentally merging the landing bundle into the main viewer bundle and silently creating coupling.

</findings>

<insights>

- High cohesion (1) — files are tightly interconnected
- Perfect cohesion (1.0) and zero coupling in a 2-file zone confirm complete isolation — the landing page has no shared dependencies with the main application.
- Zero entry points means no other zone imports from this zone, making it a true leaf; changes here carry zero blast radius.
- At 2 files this zone is below the 5-file threshold for reliable metric interpretation, but the isolation pattern is correct and should be maintained as the landing page grows.
- Zero coupling and no consumers make this a fully isolated leaf zone — an intentional design boundary that prevents landing page changes from affecting the main dashboard.
- The zone contains exactly one production file and one test file; if the landing page gains additional pages or components they should remain within this zone boundary.
- No entry points means the landing page is never imported by other zones — verify that it is loaded via a separate HTML entry point in build.js rather than through the main viewer bundle.
- landing-page has zero inbound and zero outbound cross-zone imports — it is the only zone in the web package with no coupling surface, making it safe to refactor, delete, or rebuild in isolation
- landing-page has exactly one production file — if it gains a second production file with a distinct dependency set (e.g., a sub-page or shared landing component), the zone will for the first time have a measurable internal import relationship. The first addition to this zone is the highest-leverage moment for setting zone conventions, as the zone's metric baseline will shift from trivially-perfect to genuinely-measured.
- The zone's isolation is achieved through route-level separation (separate HTML entry point in build.js) rather than gateway or spawn enforcement — a third isolation mechanism distinct from the two documented in CLAUDE.md. This 'build-entry isolation' pattern prevents any bundler-level coupling between the landing page and main application regardless of future import additions, making it structurally stronger than convention-based isolation for zones that must remain consumer-facing.
- Document the build-entry isolation mechanism explicitly in the zone's governance notes: the landing page is isolated by having a separate HTML entry point in build.js, not by import graph discipline. This prevents future maintainers from accidentally merging the landing bundle into the main viewer bundle and silently creating coupling.
- [call graph] 23 internal calls, 0 outgoing, 0 incoming (cohesion: 1, coupling: 0)

</insights>
